messages.subject: [R-sig-ins] R and 64 bit Computing
messages.body_plain:
Hi Glen,

It already does... on Linux and Mac OS. The problem on Windows is the  
lack of a reliable free 64 bit compiler; see

	http://cran.r-project.org/doc/manuals/R-admin.html#Choosing-between-32_002d-and-64_002dbit-builds

As alluded to in the R for Windows FAQ, Revolution Computing also  
provide a 64-bit version of R:

	http://www.revolution-computing.com/

Hope this helps,

Vincent

Le lun. 2 nov. ? 10:41, Meyers, Glenn G. a ?crit :

> Does anybody know if R can (or future versions will) take advantage  
> of a
> 64-bit computing environment?
>
>
>
> Glenn Meyers
>
> ISO
>
> 201-469-2333
>
>
>
> This email is intended for the recipient only.  If you are not the  
> intended recipient please disregard, and do not use the information  
> for any purpose.
>
> 	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
>

> R-SIG-insurance mailing list
